records.ety: [Gr. dys- ill, bad, + <?/ discourse, fr. <?/ to speak.]

defs: Unfavorable; not commendatory; -- opposed to eulogistic.
quotes: There is no course of conduct for which dyslogistic or eulogistic epithets may be found.
The paternity of dyslogistic -- no bantling, but now almost a centenarian -- is adjudged to that genius of common sense, Jeremy Bentham.
